perturbation.original_text: 11. Choice of Law and Venue. This Agreement and any actions under it shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania without regard to its conflicts of laws provisions. Any disputes arising under or relating to this Agreement shall be heard in the Court of Common Pleas of Allegheny County, Pennsylvania or the United States District Court for the Weste rn District of Pennsylvania. Each party hereby consents to the jurisdiction of said courts and waives any objection which they may have at any time to the jurisdiction of such courts, the laying of venue in such courts or the convenience of the forum.
perturbation.changed_text:
10. Entire A greement . This Agreement (a) is the complete agreement of the Parties concerning the subject matter hereof and supersedes any prior agreements, understanding or discussions with respect to the subject matter hereof; and (b) may not be amended or in any manner modified except by a non-electronic written instrument signed by authorized representatives of both Parties.

11. Choice of Law and Venue. Each party hereby consents to the jurisdiction of said courts and waives any objection which they may have at any time to the jurisdiction of such courts, the laying of venue in such courts or the convenience of the forum. This Agreement and any actions under it shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania without regard to its conflicts of laws provisions. Any disputes arising under or relating to this Agreement shall be heard in the Court of Common Pleas of Allegheny County, Pennsylvania or the United States District Court for the Weste rn District of Pennsylvania.
perturbation.explanation: The venue selection clause, normally a key provision, is now placed after the 'Entire Agreement' clause. The venue clause, which includes the agreement to jurisdiction and waiving objections, is now placed in a location where it may be argued that it is superseded by the 'Entire Agreement' clause because it was not present at the beginning of the agreement.
perturbation.law_explanation: By placing the forum selection/venue after the entire agreement clause, there is now ambiguity as to whether this clause is part of the 'entire agreement'. This could violate 28 U.S.C. § 1391 since enforceability of venue would be undermined.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 28 U.S. Code § 1391 - Venue generally | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 28 U.S. Code § 1391 - Venue generally U.S. Code Notes prev | next (a) Applicability of Section.— Except as otherwise provided by law— (1) this section shall govern the venue of all civil actions brought in district courts of the United States; and (2) the proper venue for a civil action shall be determined without regard to whether the action is local or transitory in nature. (b) Venue in General.— A civil action may be brought in— (1) a judicial district in which any defendant resides, if all defendants are residents of the State in which the district is located; (2) a judicial district in which a substantial part of the events or omissions giving rise to the claim occurred, or a substantial part of property that is the subject of the action is situated; or (3) if there is no district in which an action may otherwise be brought as provided in this section, any judicial district in which any defendant is subject to the court’s personal jurisdiction with respect to such action. (c) Residency.— For all venue purposes— (1) a natural person, including an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence in the United States, shall be deemed to reside in the judicial district in which that person is domiciled; (2) an entity with the capacity to sue and be sued in its common name under applicable law, whether or not incorporated, shall be deemed to reside, if a defendant, in any judicial district in which such defendant is subject to the court’s personal jurisdiction with respect to the civil action in question and, if a plaintiff, only in the judicial district in which it maintains its principal place of business; and (3) a defendant not resident in the United States may be sued in any judicial district, and the joinder of such a defendant shall be disregarded in determining where the action may be brought w
perturbation.justification: The changed text re-arranges the 'Choice of Law and Venue' clause such that it is positioned after the 'Entire Agreement' clause. This structural change introduces ambiguity as to whether the forum selection clause is part of the entire agreement, potentially undermining its enforceability. The Law Explanation supports this by stating that the placement creates ambiguity. The Perturbation Explanation also indicates that the clause may be superseded. Snippet 1 and 2 refer to 28 U.S.C. § 1391, which governs venue generally in federal courts. Although the snippets do not explicitly say what happens when there is ambiguity, the structural flaw in the changed text can impact the enforceability of the venue clause, potentially conflicting with the intention of having a clear venue as intended under 28 U.S.C. § 1391. Because the structural change in the contract creates ambiguity that may undermine the venue clause, this constitutes a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Injunctive Relief . The Parties acknowledge that the Confidential Information is a unique and valuable asset of Disclosing Party, and that disclosure in breach of this Agreement may result i n irreparable injury to Disclosing Party for which monetary damages alone would not be an adequate remedy. Therefore, the Parties agree that in the event of a breach or threatened breach of confidentiality and/or of this agreement, the Disclosing Party sh all be entitled to seek an injunction prohibiting any such breach or to specific performance or other equitable relief as a remedy available to Disclosing Party. Any such relief shall be in addition to and not in lieu of any appropriate relief in the way of monetary damages.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. No Relationship . This Agreement is intended to provide only for the handling and protection of Confidential Information. It shall not be construed as a teaming, joint venture, partnership or other similar arrangement. 8. Injunctive Relief . The Parties acknowledge that the Confidential Information is a unique and valuable asset of Disclosing Party, and that disclosure in breach of this Agreement may result i n irreparable injury to Disclosing Party for which monetary damages alone would not always be sufficient, and specific performance may not be sought. Therefore, monetary damages may be the most appropriate form of remedy. The Parties agree that in the event of a breach or threatened breach of confidentiality and/or of this agreement, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek appropriate relief in the way of monetary damages.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that monetary damages are the most appropriate remedy, this contradicts the possibility of seeking injunctive relief, which is common practice for breach of contract. It implies the parties are limited to monetary remedies even if inadequate.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Pennsylvania Rules of Civil Procedure Rule 1501
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification seeks to limit the available remedies to only monetary damages, which contrasts Pennsylvania Rules of Civil Procedure Rule 1501, where equity is allowed for breach of contract.

perturbation.justification: The original text allows for injunctive relief and specific performance. The changed text limits the available remedies to monetary damages, stating that monetary damages may be the most appropriate form of remedy and specific performance may not be sought. This directly contradicts the general principle that equitable remedies like injunctive relief are available in breach of contract cases, especially when monetary damages alone are insufficient. The Law Explanation confirms that Pennsylvania Rules of Civil Procedure Rule 1501 generally allows for equity in breach of contract cases. Even though the provided law snippets don't explicitly state this, the Perturbation Explanation notes it is common practice for breach of contract. The changed text removes a remedy that would otherwise be available, creating a legal contradiction under the 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction' type. This alteration significantly weakens the legal protection afforded to the Disclosing Party in the event of a breach.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Export Control . Each Party agrees that it will not directly or indirectly transmit, by way of trans - shipment, export, diversion or otherwise, any Confidential Information of the other Party except in accordance with any and all applicable United States expor t control laws and regulations. As an institution of higher learning, Carnegie Mellon generally performs fundamental research that is exempt from export control licensing under applicable export control laws. As a result, Carnegie Mellon typically does not wish to take receipt of export -controlled information, except as may be specifically agreed to by Carnegie Mellon and for which Carnegie Mellon has made specific arrangements. __________________ agrees that it will not provide or make accessible to Carnegie Mellon any export -controlled Confidential Information without first informing the Carnegie Mellon’s Office of Sponsored Programs of the export -controlled nature of the Confidential Information and obtaining from Carnegie Mellon’s Office of Sponsored Programs its written consent to accept such information as well as any specific instructions regarding the mechanism pursuant to which such information should be passed.
perturbation.changed_text:
5. Duration of Confidentiality. The Recipient shall maintain in confidence and shall not disclose to any person not a party hereto, unless permitted to do so under Section 2, or use or exploit in any way, without the Disclosing Party’s written agreement, any Confidential Information for a period of five (5) years from the date of disclosure of such information, unless such information ceases to be Confidential Information prior to the end of such five -year period through no fault of Recipient, or Recipient an d Disclosing Party enter into a written agreement authorizing same.

12. Export Control . Each Party agrees that it will not directly or indirectly transmit, by way of trans - shipment, export, diversion or otherwise, any Confidential Information of the other Party, it will follow the agreed upon confidentiality terms for a period of two years. As an institution of higher learning, Carnegie Mellon generally performs fundamental research that is exempt from export control licensing under applicable export control laws. As a result, Carnegie Mellon typically does not wish to take receipt of export -controlled information, except as may be specifically agreed to by Carnegie Mellon and for which Carnegie Mellon has made specific arrangements. __________________ agrees that it will not provide or make accessible to Carnegie Mellon any export -controlled Confidential Information without first informing the Carnegie Mellon’s Office of Sponsored Programs of the export -controlled nature of the Confidential Information and obtaining from Carnegie Mellon’s Office of Sponsored Programs its written consent to accept such information as well as any specific instructions regarding the mechanism pursuant to which such information should be passed.
perturbation.explanation: Here, export control section changes the duration of the confidentiality period from 5 years to 2 years. Export control is unrelated to the length of the confidentiality, therefore, placing the new duration of confidentiality here may cause confusion.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification sets a two-year limit within the export control section, contradicting the general 5-year confidentiality agreement. The export control regulations, 15 C.F.R. § 734.7(a), focuses on controlling export, but does not allow modification of time of confidentiality, so this section is being used incorrectly.

perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a two-year confidentiality term within the export control section of the contract, which contradicts the original agreement of a five-year confidentiality period. The LLM explanation states that the Export Administration Regulations (EAR), such as 15 C.F.R. § 734.7(a), focus on controlling export and do not allow modification of time of confidentiality. While the provided snippets do not explicitly state that a confidentiality duration cannot be modified in the export control section, the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation claim that doing so creates confusion and incorrectly uses the export control section. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction' definition, where a required legal disclosure (confidentiality duration) is placed in an irrelevant section (export control). The original text and changed text comparisons highlights that the duration was changed.

perturbation.original_text: 15. Term of Agreement . The term of this Agreement will be one (1) year from the Effective Date, unless it is terminated earlier by either party providing the other party with at least thirty (30) days prior written notice, or unless it is extended as mutually agreed upon by the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. No Third Part y Beneficiaries. Nothing in this Agreement, express or implied, is intended to or shall confer upon any person or entity other than Carnegie Mellon and _________ any right, benefit or remedy of any nature whatsoever under or by reason of this Agreement. 15. Term of Agreement . This agreement can only be terminated by written agreement by both parties, in addition to that, there is a lifetime guarantee for the exchange of confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes the term of the agreement and adds a lifetime guarantee for confidential information. This contradicts the stated one-year term. The modification replaces how the term can be terminated and now requires both parties to agree to the termination. The guarantee is also not very specific.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification changes the terms to require both parties to agree to termination, and provides a lifetime guarantee, going against the one-year term, and may be unenforceable depending on context 13 Pa.C.S.A. § 2209, dealing with good faith modification, due to ambiguity on when this applies.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a lifetime guarantee for confidential information and alters the termination clause, requiring mutual agreement for termination instead of the original 30-day written notice. This directly contradicts the originally agreed-upon one-year term. The Law Explanation further explains that this change may be unenforceable. The Perturbation Explanation highlights that the new guarantee is not specific, making it difficult to enforce. Even without direct supporting snippets, the changes introduced directly undermine the original agreement and create a contradiction. The structural flaw involves changing a central part of the contract. Therefore, a contradiction exists.
